algoritm text top: This algorithm is designed for text encryption and operates on the basis of processing each letter separately. In the algorithm, it is assumed that the alphabet consists of 26 letters, and all calculations are performed according to module 26.
algoritm text bottom: The encryption process is performed according to the following formula: Here Eᵢ is the alphabetical sequence number of the encrypted letter. xᵢ is the alphabetical sequence number of a letter in plaintext, defined, for example, as A=0, B=1,..., Z=26. i is the sequence number of the letter within a word or text. K is the encryption key, which is a pre-agreed integer value. During the algorithm's operation, each letter in the plaintext is converted into a number, to which the location index of the letter and the key value are added accordingly. The resulting result is determined by the remainder, divided by 26. Thus, plaintext is converted into encrypted text. This approach, unlike conventional Caesar-type encryption, generates a different shift value for each letter. This relatively increases the security level of the encrypted text and reduces repetitive patterns. One of the important aspects of this algorithm is that if the same letters are located in different positions, their encrypted appearance will also be different. For example, in plaintext, the same letter is repeated in different places, resulting in a different result each time. This feature provides a certain degree of protection against conventional cryptanalysis methods based on frequency analysis. Another advantage of the algorithm is that it is simple in terms of calculation and works quickly and efficiently in software. At the same time, by changing the key value, the encryption result can be completely transformed. This allows the algorithm to be applied flexibly at different security levels. This encryption method is convenient for use mainly in educational processes, in explaining algorithms, and in cases where it is necessary to hide simple information.
